sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id UInt32, content String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (1, 'hello world'), (2, 'world of SQL'), (3, 'fuzzy testing');
SELECT multiSearchFirstPositionUTF8(content, ['wor', 'ld', 'ello']) AND id > 1 FROM test_strings WHERE content IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, multiSearchFirstPositionUTF8(content, ['SQL', 'fuzzy']) AS position FROM test_strings WHERE position > 0 GROUP BY content HAVING position < 10;
SELECT content, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE multiSearchFirstPositionUTF8(content, ['hello', 'SQL']) > 0) AS match_counter FROM test_strings;
SELECT multiSearchFirstPositionUTF8(content, [CASE WHEN id = 1 THEN 'world' ELSE 'bye' END, 'hello']) AS found_position FROM test_strings WHERE content LIKE '%hello%' AND found_position > 0;
CREATE TABLE test_strings2 (id UInt32, part String DEFAULT 'test') ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO test_strings2 VALUES (1, 'testing relationships'), (2, NULL), (3, 'fuzzy edges');
SELECT multiSearchFirstPositionUTF8(content, ['fuzzy', 'edges']) AS position FROM test_strings2 WHERE position IS NOT NULL AND position <> 0;

SELECT a.content, b.content, multiSearchFirstPositionUTF8(a.content, [b.content]) AS match_position FROM test_strings a JOIN test_strings2 b ON multiSearchFirstPositionUTF8(b.content, ['testing']) > 0;
